mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, consumer confidence stabilizes and wage growth expectations anchor, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic expansion, which compresses credit spreads and lifts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI exceeds 240 and the federal funds rate remains elevated, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing real yields higher and compressing equity multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of triggered episodes, or the signal loses predictive power when controlling for the 10-year Treasury yield.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins and revenue bases, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month forward window.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.15, or the signal generates negative returns in more than 60% of expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent indicates rising factory output and capacity utilization, which boosts industrial sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes after adjusting for oil price shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and an unemployment rate below 5.0 percent confirms a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending while avoiding recessionary dynamics, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for consumer credit growth.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in residential investment exceeding 5 percent signals a housing sector downturn that precedes broader economic contractions, increasing recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over a baseline recession probability model.
